positioning: Prototype for quantitative ML leakage auditing with a grounded live sklearn audit-replay benchmark.

scripts_used.purpose: Runs live sklearn scenarios through AuditedEstimator, AuditedPipeline, and PipelineAuditor, then writes grounded JSON results.

grounded_claims.claim: The repository contains executable sklearn audit wrappers that record live stage events through AuditedEstimator, AuditedPipeline, and PipelineAuditor.
On the live audit recovery benchmark, a simple checker over live audit logs separated 4 leaky and 4 clean sklearn scenarios with TP=4, TN=4, FP=0, FN=0.
In the four paired real-dataset scenarios of the recovery benchmark, leaky and clean variants had identical held-out scores, so structural audit evidence can matter even when score deltas are absent.
In a synthetic random-label feature-selection stress test, fitting SelectKBest before the split increased test accuracy from 0.5833 to 0.7417 (+0.1583 absolute).
Measured audited-wrapper overhead was near-1x on two representative clean scenarios: 1.115x on clean_scaler_iris and 1.014x on clean_pca_wine.
The current grounded evaluation validates the live sklearn wrapper and replay layer, not the full taintflow audit CLI.

theory_claims_retained.claim: The repository implements a partition-taint lattice and worklist analyzer for quantitative leakage analysis.
The paper's quantitative story is best read as a research direction supported by executable wrappers and partial analysis components, rather than as a fully validated end-to-end product claim.

removed_story_elements: Descriptor-benchmark perfect recall / zero-FPR / perfect-F1 claims.
Broad head-to-head benchmark claims against external tooling.
